approval_notes: Owner-approved F004 plan. Slices 1 (safe fetch guard + SourceAdapter contract, PR #14), 2 (migration 0004 + run_scan orchestration, PR #15) and 3 (reconciliation, PR #16) are merged to main. This contract governs Slice 4 ONLY.

Standing autonomy boundaries: may branch/implement/test/commit/push/open ONE PR; may NOT merge, flip any passes flag, add a runtime/npm dependency, start Slice 5, or touch the offer_version immutability trigger.
F004 stays passes:false after this slice. A fresh-context Level 2 slice evaluation reviews Slice 4 before Slice 5 proceeds.
objective: Add two new stdlib-only source adapters behind the Slice 1 SourceAdapter contract and the Slice 1 Fetcher seam: an RSS/Atom adapter (xml.etree) and a static-doc/HTML adapter (html.parser) whose provider-specific selectors live in a declarative extraction_profile (quota_document, pricing_document) rather than in code. Each adapter implements all seven contract methods (discover/fetch/canonicalize/extract/validate/evidence/health), produces candidate-only facts with evidence locations, and NEVER guesses a value or raises an uncaught exception. Malformed or partial input yields a handled outcome (a rejected/partial candidate that validate flags, i.e. a captured validation failure surfaced to the ScanRun as errors_count, or UNKNOWN/None facts) - never a crash and never a fabricated value. Both adapters are registered by source.adapter_type in the scan ADAPTER_REGISTRY and reach the network only through the injected SafeFetcher, so a non-allowlisted URL is refused before any connection. Per-type contract fixtures (unchanged/changed/malformed/partial/contradictory) with expected candidate facts + evidence locations back the adapter tests.
scope: apps/api/app/ingest/adapters/__init__.py: new adapters sub-package exporting RssFeedAdapter and HtmlDocAdapter.

apps/api/app/ingest/adapters/rss.py: RSS 2.0 + Atom adapter using xml.etree.ElementTree; each feed item/entry -> one candidate; DTD/entity guard (reject DOCTYPE) as XXE/billion-laughs defence-in-depth; ParseError -> rejected candidate; missing identity/link -> partial UNKNOWN facts flagged by validate.

apps/api/app/ingest/adapters/html.py: static-doc/HTML adapter using html.parser.HTMLParser; a generic table-walking engine driven entirely by a declarative HtmlExtractionProfile (record table selector + column->fact-field map + value coercions); HTML_EXTRACTION_PROFILES registry with quota_document + pricing_document; missing table -> rejected candidate, missing required column/cell -> partial UNKNOWN.
apps/api/app/ingest/scan.py: register 'rss' and 'html' in ADAPTER_REGISTRY (html resolves source.parser_profile against HTML_EXTRACTION_PROFILES); no other orchestration change.
apps/api/app/ingest/__init__.py: export the two adapters and the HTML profile registry/type.

tests/unit/test_adapter_rss.py + tests/unit/test_adapter_html.py: a per-type contract test driving all 7 methods and asserting expected candidate facts + evidence against fixtures; malformed -> handled (no crash, no guess); partial -> partial/UNKNOWN; contradictory -> both candidates extracted unresolved (adapters never reconcile); a non-allowlisted URL is refused via the shared fetcher.
out_of_scope: Any change to run_scan/reconcile logic beyond adding the two registry entries (Slice 2/3 invariants unchanged).
Creating or mutating offer/offer_version (no publication path); the offer_version immutability trigger is untouched.
Wiring adapters into the worker/scheduler/API runtime; DB schema changes / new migration.
Candidate -> service/offer DB matching; reconciliation/contradiction resolution (that is Slice 3's pure logic, exercised elsewhere).
Residual R1 (DNS-rebinding IP pinning) from Slice 1 - explicitly deferred.
Any new runtime or npm dependency (requirements-sync guard stays green; stdlib xml.etree + html.parser only). Marking F004 passes:true. Advancing to Slice 5.
acceptance_criteria: Contract-per-type: for each of RSS and HTML, the 7 contract methods run end-to-end through the offline FixtureFetcher and produce the expected candidate-only facts and evidence locations for the unchanged and changed fixtures (changed differs from unchanged in a material fact).

Malformed-per-type: a malformed source yields a handled outcome (a rejected candidate flagged by validate, i.e. a captured validation failure; errors surfaced, no persisted candidate) with NO fabricated offer value and NO uncaught exception.
Partial-per-type: a partial source yields candidate facts with UNKNOWN (None) for the missing material fields (never guessed) and validate flags the gap.

Contradictory-per-type: a source presenting two conflicting values for the same identity's material fact yields both candidates extracted faithfully and unresolved; the adapter performs no reconciliation or auto-resolution.

Candidate-only: every produced fact has an adapter-assignable verification_state (never 'verified'); adapters import only app.ingest.base + app.ingest.fetch and reach the network solely through the injected Fetcher.
Safe fetch: a URL whose host is not in the policy allowlist is refused (DisallowedHostError) by the shared fetcher before any connection, exercised per adapter.
Both adapters are registered in ADAPTER_REGISTRY by adapter_type and build via build_adapter without touching the orchestration body.

Regression green: scripts/test.ps1 and scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit pass offline (ruff, format, pytest, prettier, eslint, detect-secrets, pip-audit, npm audit) including the requirements-sync guard (no new dependency).
A fresh-context independent Level 2 evaluator records an adversarial verdict for Slice 4 before any status change; F004 remains passes:false.

integration_commands: Not required: Slice 4 touches no DB path (run_scan body unchanged; only two registry entries added). The existing live-DB integration suites remain the Slice 2/3 evidence.

risks.data_migration: None. No migration and no schema change; only additive Python modules plus two ADAPTER_REGISTRY entries. offer_version and its immutability trigger are untouched.
risks.security_privacy: Adapters reach the network only through the injected SafeFetcher, so the Slice 1 SSRF/allowlist/scheme/MIME/size/redirect guards apply unchanged; a non-allowlisted host is refused pre-connection. The RSS adapter rejects DOCTYPE/entity declarations (XXE / billion-laughs defence-in-depth) and xml.etree resolves no external entities. Adapters produce candidate-only facts; official-only-evidence and no-publication-path invariants are enforced by run_scan (unchanged).
risks.portability: Pure standard-library Python (xml.etree.ElementTree, html.parser); no new dependency; amd64/arm64 unaffected.
risks.zero_cost_safety: No offer data is published; adapters emit candidate facts only; malformed/partial never guesses a value ('unknown is better than guessed'); no Z0/free claim is asserted. Fixtures use a synthetic 'example' provider so no unsupported real free-tier claim is introduced.
risks.regression: run_scan and reconcile logic are unchanged apart from two additive registry entries, so Slice 2's zero-change_event-on-scan and official-only-evidence invariants and Slice 3's reconciliation still hold.
risks.supply_chain: No new Python or npm dependency; requirements-sync guard stays green.

notes: Owner approved the F004 plan and directed Slice 4 implementation, then stop for a fresh-context Level 2 slice evaluation before Slice 5.
